channel.title: Foreshore
channel.description: Trend-intelligence for the wellness sector — what the tide is bringing in, published through the dual-native engine.

item.title: The Game Night That Started Keeping Score
item.description: Metro Vancouver has mapped 77 mahjong venues with peak search velocity for the format's league vocabulary — a geographic cluster suggesting a structured, standings-based version of the game is forming as neighbourhood infrastructure before any national outlet has named it.

item.title: The Rebrand That Comes With a Blood Panel
item.description: "Longevity clinic" is consolidating as a label ahead of any regulatory definition, and the shift is showing up independently in venue signage and search behavior across Metro Vancouver — a vocabulary change that lets existing med-spa and wellness operators reprice as diagnostic, membership-based medicine without changing what they actually do inside.

item.title: The Race That Rebuilt the Gym Floor
item.description: Hyrox's visible story is a structured fitness-racing format crossing into North American mainstream; the real business is the training economy it generates — equipment retrofits, programming licenses, race-prep coaching, and specialist nutrition serving a customer cohort that organises spending around a race calendar.

item.title: The Ice Bath That Learned to Scale
item.description: Cold plunge has crossed from high-income biohacker home installation to commercial venue standard — 120 venues across Metro Vancouver and peak search velocity mark the transition point, with the service-layer opportunity sitting in the stripped-down protocol studio that sells outcomes rather than ambiance.

item.title: The City That Already Built the Bathhouse
item.description: Metro Vancouver has mapped 170 sauna venues with peak search velocity — a geographic cluster suggesting the social bathing club model is forming as urban infrastructure before national hospitality capital or media has named it.

item.title: Breath-work practitioners: Shadow Watch
item.description: The queue found one live signal for Breath-work practitioners.

item.title: The Phone No One Manufactures Anymore
item.description: The corded landline revival is culturally visible but supply-side hollow — quality new corded phones are nearly impossible to source at retail, telecom infrastructure is being actively decommissioned, and the business opportunity sits with whoever rebuilds the supply chain before the aesthetic locks in.

item.title: Brazilian Jiu Jitsu Lifestyle: Shadow Watch
item.description: The queue found one live signal for Brazilian Jiu Jitsu Lifestyle.

item.title: Hormone Health Testing: Shadow Watch
item.description: The queue found one live signal for Hormone Health Testing.

item.title: The Call You Had to Stay For
item.description: A single Dazed Digital editorial marks the domestic corded landline as an emerging wellness object among digital-burnout youth — distinct from dumb phone substitution in that it anchors the user to a room rather than reducing mobile capability.

item.title: The Bruise You Chose
item.description: A single Dazed Digital editorial marks bruise-imitation tattoos as an emerging aesthetic category at the convergence of damaged-body aesthetics, hyperrealism technique expansion, and somatic identity marking; confirmation within eight weeks would open a narrow but defensible opportunity in specialized tattoo ink supply.

item.title: When the Kimono Gets a Fashion Week Co-Sign
item.description: BJJ practitioners are a disproportionately high-income, brand-loyal demographic that fashion has been slow to recognize as taste-makers; the Needles x Albino & Preto collab signals that recognition has arrived, and the lifestyle layer between the mat and the street remains unbuilt.

item.title: The Accidental Wellness Chamber
item.description: The phrase 'wellness hack' is migrating onto cinema-going among young audiences, relocating the cinema ticket from the entertainment budget to the wellness budget — a reframing that operators have not yet named or productized.

item.title: The Bat and Ball That Four Houses Packed This Season
item.description: Loro Piana, Hermès, Bottega Veneta, and Chanel have independently converged on cooperative beach bat-and-ball — frescobol/matkot — in their 2026 resort aesthetics.

item.title: The Back Bar Nobody Stocks
item.description: The sober-curious caffeine cohort is large enough that hospitality operators now face a procurement gap — no distributor has assembled an on-trade premium non-alcoholic portfolio as a packaged menu offer, creating a B2B supply-chain opportunity twelve to eighteen months ahead of mature infrastructure.

item.title: The Sober Drink Gets a Stimulant
item.description: The phrase 'sober-curious' is migrating from alcohol-abstinence framing into caffeine-adoption framing, consolidating iced matcha, wellness energy drinks, and flavored pouches under a single buyer identity — the whitespace is a social-occasion caffeine brand built for hospitality distribution, a channel no current player owns.

item.title: The Wrong Student in the Right Studio
item.description: Ceramics studios built on aesthetic-era demand are running a vocational trades curriculum, creating structural dropout that threatens re-enrollment economics — the opportunity is an outcome-first beginner format for the buyer who wants the object, not the craft mastery.

item.title: The Tiny Room at the End of the Feed
item.description: A Canadian reality TV format is driving new entrants into miniature crafting from outside the existing hobby base; the beginner onboarding and supply infrastructure for this non-hobbyist buyer does not yet exist.

item.title: The Opt-Out Class
item.description: Tech media professionals are normalizing smartphone abandonment — not experimenting with it — and the onboarding infrastructure for the second wave of adopters has not been built.

item.title: The Byproduct Running the Beauty Counter
item.description: The 'salmon facial' trend is celebrity-amplified noise on top of a decade-old South Korean clinical ingredient; the boring business is the Western distribution and training layer between concentrated Korean PDRN biotech manufacturers and the fragmented US/EU med-spa market.

item.title: Everything Is Automated Except the Label
item.description: AI penetration of consumer production has made human involvement legible as a premium claim — 'made by humans' is beginning to surface as marketing vocabulary in wellness, but without any verification infrastructure to make the label stick or defensible.

item.title: What the Forum Boys Found on the Luxury Shelf
item.description: The word 'looksmaxxing' has migrated from incel-adjacent forums into premium fashion media, handing male grooming and fragrance brands a new buyer-identity frame — optimization rather than vanity — that removes the historical friction in selling appearance products to young men.

item.title: Learning to Hold the Door
item.description: Celebrity adoption of death doula training is the rich-people-beta signal for a profession about to be formalized — the near-term business is the credentialing and vetting infrastructure, not the doulas themselves.

item.title: The cleanup economy
item.description: The mature GLP-1 story is the drug; the next business is the maintenance layer that cleans up after rapid weight loss, beginning with skin and aesthetics, where the buyer already has a named problem and the seller still speaks the old vocabulary.

item.title: The GLP-1 Skin Trade Is the Maintenance Layer
item.description: The live Foreshore queue saw duplicate GLP-1 skin labels from a May 19 RSS cycle; the commercial read is a maintenance layer around rapid weight loss, not a confirmed demand wave yet.
